title: QUIC server-side 0-RTT receive closed-gate verification
verification_method: Focused requirement-home tests with positive, negative, fuzz, and public-surface guard coverage plus SpecTrace JSON validation.
scope: Verify that the current managed server endpoint/runtime path keeps early-data admission closed for unrouted 0-RTT datagrams without creating connections, streams, application delivery, server-side ZeroRtt open material, or public early-data promises.
preconditions: REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S17P2P5P1-0005 remains the Retry bootstrap owner for 0-RTT-triggered Retry issuance.
REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S17P2P5P1-0006 remains the RFC 9000 discard-permission owner.
No separate server-side early-data admission or anti-replay owner exists yet.
procedure: Run the focused REQ-QUIC-CRT-0149 requirement-home tests.
Confirm the server runtime reports early-data admission closed.
Confirm structurally valid unrouted Version 1 0-RTT datagrams are classified as application-data packets but remain unroutable at the endpoint boundary.
Fuzz randomized unrouted 0-RTT datagrams to prove the disposition remains non-admitting.
Confirm exported public members do not expose early-data admission or anti-replay promises.
Validate the edited canonical SpecTrace JSON and run whitespace diff checks.
expected_result: Server-side 0-RTT receive remains a closed internal gate for unrouted datagrams: no connection is created, no streams are opened, no application data is delivered, no server-side ZeroRtt open material is installed, Retry bootstrap remains separately owned, and public early-data or anti-replay promises remain absent.

supplemental_sections.heading: Acceptance Criteria
supplemental_sections.content:
- Server runtime early-data admission remains closed.
- Unrouted Version 1 0-RTT datagrams return `Unroutable` with no connection handle and no endpoint handling side effect.
- Randomized structurally valid unrouted 0-RTT datagrams keep the same non-admitting disposition.
- The public surface still exposes no early-data admission or anti-replay promise.
- Retry bootstrap behavior remains under its existing RFC 9000 requirement and is not broadened.

status_summary: Passed for the focused REQ-QUIC-CRT-0149 requirement-home coverage and canonical SpecTrace JSON validation.
